questions.question:
Two diatomic gases A and B are taken in a closed vessel in equal proportion by their moles. The molecular mass of gas A (MA) is less than the molecular mass of gas B (MB). Which of the following statement is INCORRECT?
 Average molecular mass of the mixture is lesser than MB but greater than MA.
 If gas A is N2 and gas B is O2, then the average molecular mass of the mixture will be 30.
 On adding one mole of gas B to the vessel, the average molecular mass of the mixture increases.
 If the gases are taken in unequal proportions by their mass, then the average molecular mass of the mixture will be greater than MB.
questions.question_raw:
Two diatomic gases A and B are taken in a closed vessel in equal proportion by their moles. The molecular mass of gas A (MA) is less than the molecular mass of gas B (MB). Which of the following statement is INCORRECT?

  1. Average molecular mass of the mixture is lesser than MB but greater than MA.
  2. If gas A is N2 and gas B is O2, then the average molecular mass of the mixture will be 30.
  3. On adding one mole of gas B to the vessel, the average molecular mass of the mixture increases.
  4. If the gases are taken in unequal proportions by their mass, then the average molecular mass of the mixture will be greater than MB.
